title: قرار رقم 26 لسنة 2009: بشأن إعتماد وإصدار تعليمات خدمة نقل أرقام الهواتف المتحركة

provisions.content:
(8) of Federal Law by Decree no 3 of 2003 regarding the Organizationof the Telecommunication Sector grants the TRA the power, inter alia, to issueregulations, orders, resolutions and procedures in relation to Mobile NumberPortability.2.2. TheTRA has issued the TRA's Policy for Number Portability.3.Definitions:3.1All words, terms and phrases used in these Instructions shall have the meaningsascribed to them, if any, in either the Federal Law by Decree no 3 of 2003regarding the Organization of the Telecommunication Sector, the TRA's Policy forNumber Portability, or the TRA's Policy for National Numbering Plan, in eachcase as amended or replaced from time to time as the case may be, unlessotherwise provided for in these Instructions.3.2For the purposes of these Instructions, the following terms shall have themeanings ascribed to them immediately below:ServiceCommencementDate The date which the TRA designatesas the date on which Licensees shall provide Mobile Number Portability toSubscribers.RFPHas the meaning ascribed to it in paragraph 1.1.IGNPHas the meaning ascribed to it in paragraph 1.1.LicenseesEmirates Telecommunications Corporation (Etisalat) and Emirates IntegratedTelecommunication Company (du).MNPContract The Development and Managed Services Agreement between the MNPProvider and the TRA for the provision and operation of Mobile Number PortingSystem so as to enable MNP to be provided to Subscribers in the UAE.MNPProvider The entity appointed to build and operate the Mobile NumberPorting System under the MNP Contract including all associated hardware,software, communications systems and other infrastructure items.MobileNumber Portability or MNP The capability or service whereby a Subscriberwho is being provided with a Telecommunications Service for mobilecommunications services in association or connection with a particular MobileNumber, can terminate the contract with the Licensee providing thatTelecommunications Service, but continue to use that Mobile Number inassociation or connection with the provision of a Telecommunications Service formobile communications services by another Licensee.NewLicensees An entity licensed to provide Telecommunication Services in theUAE after the coming into force of these Instructions.PortWith respect to a specific Number, the retention by the relevant Subscriber ofthe use of that Number while following a change in the identity of the Licenseeproviding the relevant Telecommunications Service, and the terms" Porting "," Ported " and" Porting Process " shall all havecorresponding meanings.PrepaidSubscriber A Subscriber for Telecommunications Service where the relevantLicensee allows the Subscriber to pay for the Telecommunications Service inadvance of the actual use of the Telecommunications Services.PostpaidSubscriber A Subscriber for Telecommunications Service where the relevantLicensee allows the Subscriber to pay for a significant portion of theTelecommunications Services subsequent to the actual use of theTelecommunications Service.PortingProcess Specification The detailed set of specifications describing theprocess for the provision of Mobile Number Portability in the UAE, set out asAnnex 1 to these Instructions, or as may be published by the TRA from time totime.MNPRegulatory Fees The fees and other charges imposed from time to time bythe TRA on the Licensees (and if appropriate, on any New Licensees, or on any ofthem), referred to in paragraph 4.6.4.Instructions4.1The Licensees shall implement MNP in their respective networks and systems, andmake it commercially available to their respective Subscribers, in accordancewith the Regulatory Framework, by no later than the Service CommencementDate.4.2Licensees shall fully co-operate with other Licensees, the MNP Provider, and theTRA in order to ensure that MNP is commercially available to each of theLicensee's Subscribers by the Service Commencement Date.4.3The Licensees are obliged to run a national advertising campaign concerning theavailability of Mobile Number Portability in the UAE, to the satisfaction of theTRA.4.4Licensees shall not, nor shall they attempt to interfere, restrict, prohibit,delay or frustrate the attempts of the MNP Provider to carry out its contractualobligations to the TRA under the MNP Contract.4.5With relation to the costs associated with the implementation of Mobile NumberPortability, the following shall apply:4.5.1 theLicensees shall bear all of their own setup and maintenance costs in relation tointerfaces with the Centralized Data Base, their Mobile Number portabilitymanagement team and all other internal and external costs associated with thepreparation of their networks for the introduction and provision of MobileNumber Portability;4.5.2 theLicensees shall bear all costs associated with the establishment and operationof the Mobile Number Porting System (PROVIDED THAT the TRA may provideotherwise, where for example, any New Licensees become subject to theseInstructions);4.5.3 thecosts associated with the establishment and operation of the Mobile NumberPorting System shall be apportioned equally between the Licensees, and may berecovered by the TRA from the Licensees in accordance with the RegulatoryFramework in place from time to time;4.5.4 theLicensees shall bear their own additional communication costs and database querycosts incurred in relation to the provision of Telecommunication Services toSubscribers who have had the relevant Mobile Numbers Ported;4.5.5 theDonor Licensee shall not be entitled to recover any of its costs incurred inrelation to Number Portability from the Recipient Licensee, and nor shall theRecipient Licensee be entitled to recover any of its costs from the DonorLicensee; and4.5.6 theDonor Licensee shall not be entitled to recover from the relevant Subscriber,any of its costs associated with the Porting of a Number. For the avoidance ofdoubt, nothing in these instructions will deny the Donor Licensee the right toenforce any contractual rights that it may have against a Subscriber, for earlytermination of a contract for the provision of Telecommunications ServicesPROVIDED THAT the Donor Licensee does not thereby recover or seek to recover anycosts associated with the Porting of the relevant Number.4.6The TRA shall from time to time, in its absolute discretion either4.6.1recover from the Licensees (and if applicable, from any New Licensees); or4.6.2require payment in advance from the Licensees (and if applicable, from any NewLicensees),theamount of any costs, charges, fees and expenses incurred directly or indirectlyby the TRA as a result of the introduction of MNP into the UAE, including anycosts, charges, fees, and expenses incurred by the TRA under the MNP Contract.The TRA may do this through any mechanism it sees fit, including but not limitedto the imposition of MNP Regulatory Fees.TheLicensees (and if applicable, any New Licensees) shall pay any MNP RegulatoryFees strictly as provided for by the TRA.4.7Unless specified otherwise by the TRA in writing, the time taken between arequest to port a Mobile Number being received and the commencement of theprovision of service on the Recipient Licensee's network should be less than one(1) working day.4.8The Donor Licensee shall only refuse to accept a porting request from aSubscriber for the reasons set out in the Porting Process Specification.4.9It is the responsibility of the Recipient Licensee to take all reasonable stepsto ensure that any eligibility requirements for Subscribers to be able to PortMobile Numbers (which eligibility requirements may be notified by the TRA to theLicensees) are met.4.10 .Prepaid Subscribers shall be able to Port without any restriction related to thesettlement of any debts owed to the Donor Licensee by the Prepaid Subscriber.Furthermore, the Donor Licensee shall not reject Porting requests solely on thebasis that the Postpaid Subscriber has outstanding debt PROVIDED THAT they arenot subject to any kind of service suspension due to unpaid bills related to theNumber to be Ported.4.11 .Postpaid Subscribers shall be able, during the Porting process, to receive allfree of charge services, to make and receive all calls to geographic and MobileNumbers within the UAE and international destinations, including national andinternational SMS. However, Licensees are not required to make Shared RevenueServices available to Postpaid Subscribers during the Porting process.4.12There shall be no limitation on the service features available to PrepaidSubscribers by either the Donor Licensee or the Recipient Licensee, asappropriate, as a result of the Porting process PROVIDED THAT:a. thosePrepaid Subscribers have sufficient credit with the relevant Licensee at therelevant time; andb. in thecase of international roaming services, the relevant Licensee is able to applyinstant billing/deduction from the Prepaid Subscriber's credit.4.13The Donor Licensee shall not use information gained from Number Portability formarketing purposes, or to contact Subscribers or potential Subscribers, whetherin relation to sales and marketing strategies including to those referred to as"retention" or "winback".4.14The Licensees shall report the following to the TRA at periods determined by theTRA:4.151 the number of requests Recipient Licensees receive for the porting ofindividual telephone numbers or telephone number groups;4.15.2the number of requests for porting made by the Recipient Licensee that have beenejected by the Donor Licensee, including the reasons for rejection;4.14.3the number of ports which were not effected within the time scales agreedbetween the Licensees and approved by the TRA; and4.15.4any other requests for information that the TRA may make from time totime,5.Effective DateTheseInstructions will be effective from the issuance date.6.PublicationThe TRAreserves the right to modify, amend or supplement these Instructions at any timein its sole discretion in accordance with its formal procedures. The TRA alsoreserves the right and at its sole discretion to make these Instructionspublicly available.7.Acknowledgement and Compliance7.1 TheLicensees shall each notify the TRA in writing of their receipt of theseInstructions on the date received.7.2 TheLicensees shall each notify the TRA in writing of their compliance with theseInstructions,');" />
/>');" />1.Introduction1.1 Allwords, terms and phrases used in these Instructions shall have the meaningsascribed to them, if any, in either the Federal Law by Decree no 3 of 2003regarding the Organization of the Telecommunication Sector, the TRA's Policy forNumber Portability, or the TRA's Policy for National Numbering Plan, in eachcase as amended or replaced from time to time as the case may be, unlessotherwise provided for in these Instructions,1.2 Thepurpose of this document is to define the Porting Process for Mobile NumberPortability in UAE.1.3 Thisdocument refers only to Mobile Number Portability, and does not cover fixedNumber Portability, or any other form of Number or service portability.1.4Without limitation to anything else provided for in this document, the NationalPorting System shall allow the following Porting features:-“Change,” allowing the RL to change information in the originalPorting Request; and-"Cancel," which allows the RL to cancel the Porting Request.The abovefeatures shall be made available with the option to enable or disablethem.1.5 TheNational Porting System shall utilize easily configurable timers to identifyconstraints that govern the order of execution of the Porting Processes, andhelp ensure that Porting Requests are implemented in a timely manner. Differenttimer values may be defined for each of the timers according to suchcharacteristics as installation type, or number-type. It shall be possible toconfigure and change timing values at any time throughout the operation ofNational Porting System.1.6 TheNational Porting System shall acknowledge each message received from either theDL or the RL. Such
